finding: Official NExT-GQA dataset not present locally at official scale. Expected 567 val / 990 test videos, 3358 / 5553 questions. Local repo only holds 91 mp4 files under eval/data/nextqa/NExTVideo_flat.
eval/data/nextqa/gsub_val.json and eval/data/nextqa/gsub_test.json are byte-identical (same SHA-256), so no independent official test-split grounding annotation exists in this repo.
gsub_val.json contains 999 video keys, not the official 567 -- cannot be confirmed as the authentic official validation grounding split without an external reference copy to diff against.
val.csv (NExT-QA multiple-choice question file) references 1000 unique video IDs but only 91 have a corresponding local mp4 -- most referenced videos were never downloaded to this checkout.
evidence: csv unique video ids = 1000; local mp4 files = 91

finding: User explicitly authorized proceeding with the existing 89-video / 255-event non-official subset (data/nextqa_exp1a/nextqa_exp1a_subset.csv) as an explicitly-labeled placeholder dataset for setup/dry-run purposes only. This does not resolve the BLOCKING findings above; official-scale dataset acquisition remains an open task before PRE_BENCHMARK_SETUP_READY.json can be issued.
